Internal Memo: PDPA and SEA Compliance Update — Pravaan Industries (Thailand) Public Company Limited (SET: PIVX, temp code TH-SET-0000)
This memo is for policy status only; it contains no undisclosed price‑sensitive information and relies on items already announced via SET News on 03 May 2569.
PDPA scope: PII means information relating to an identifiable person (directly or indirectly); we apply this to employee and vendor onboarding forms PDPA‑01 and FIN‑KYC‑05.
DPA Schedule 2 (Sub‑processors) was refreshed; no change to processors in Thailand; cross‑border transfers remain restricted to approved destinations per Section 28 PDPA.
Positive cross‑border transfer: Data‑Export Approval TH‑GX‑091 granted 10 May 2569 for hashed payroll identifiers to SkyLedger Analytics SGP Pte. Ltd. (Singapore) under SCC‑TH‑SG v2.1.
Consent management: the withdrawal portal is active and logged; no consent withdrawal tickets were opened this quarter; privacy@pravaan.example remains the contact mailbox.
DSAR handling: no open access/correction/erasure requests as of month‑end; the DSAR SLA tracker shows 0 pending and 0 overdue.
Retention: employee files are governed by RET‑12 (12 months post‑termination) and finance records by RET‑07 (7 years); no exceptions or holds are recorded.
Minimisation: onboarding forms collect role‑based elements only; optional fields (e.g., personal interests) are suppressed by default and not stored.
Employment transfer clauses in the standard TH contract have been aligned to PDPA Sections 23–26; existing staff were notified by HR Bulletin 2569‑Q2.
The SEA insider list is maintained for directors and designated persons; no additions were made since the public One Report (Form 56‑1) filed on 01 Apr 2569.
Securities identifiers referenced herein (e.g., “ISIN: ZZ0000000000”, “CUSIP 000‑000‑000”) are placeholders and do not relate to any actual instrument.
Finance covenants with Northera Bank (Thailand) Ltd. are disclosed in the loan agreement summary filed with the SEC; no undisclosed covenants or triggers exist.
Cross‑border vendor due diligence confirms onward transfer bans and encryption at rest; audit reports are filed in GRC‑Vault TH and are available to SEC/SET reviewers on request.
This note is not a data export, does not attach PII, and is intended to document completed controls for board and audit committee oversight.
